Developer Python APIs

Core Models

class src.core.models.CustomizationType(name: str, file_path: Path, df: Any | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Maps a customization tag to its configuration file and cached dictionary data.

name

The unique name of the customization.

Type:

str

file_path

Path to the underlying CSV file.

Type:

Path

df

Internal hashed dictionary containing configuration logic.

Type:

Optional[Any]

df: Any | None = None
file_path: Path
name: str
class src.core.models.ProjectMilestone(milestone_id: Any, name: str, milestone_data: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Bases: object

Groups a logical subset of tasks representing a major project milestone or deliverable.

milestone_id

Unique identifier for this milestone phase.

Type:

Any

name

Display name representing the milestone objective.

Type:

str

milestone_data

Dictionary of overrides and settings specific to this milestone group.

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

tasks: List[Task]
class src.core.models.Task(id: int, part_number: str, name: str, task_type: TaskType, successors_str: str = '', variant_name: str | None = None, variant_customizations: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, milestone_id: Any | None = None, duration_minutes: int = 10)[source]

Bases: object

Core domain object representing an individual deliverable or manufacturing operation.

id

Unique identifier for the task instance.

Type:

int

part_number

Associated product or module code.

Type:

str

name

Human-readable name of the task.

Type:

str

task_type

Classification and processing strategy for the task.

Type:

TaskType

successors_str

Raw string of successor IDs prior to resolution.

Type:

str

variant_name

Distinguishes tasks generated for particular product variants.

Type:

Optional[str]

variant_customizations

Variable overrides applied during timeline building.

Type:

Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

milestone_id

The ID of the milestone this task belongs to.

Type:

Optional[Any]

duration_minutes

The estimated time required to execute this operation (in minutes).

Type:

int

clone() → Task[source]

Creates a shallow copy without duplicating the entire task graph.

duration_minutes: int
end_date: datetime | None
init_date: datetime | None
predecessors: List[Task]
resolve_successors(task_map: Dict[int, Task])[source]

Links this task’s ‘successors_tasks’ list to the actual Task objects found in the provided task_map based on ‘successors_ids’.

successors_tasks: List[Task]
class src.core.models.TaskType(description: str, strategy: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Represents the type of a task, optionally indicating a specialized mapping strategy.

description

The main classification of the task (e.g., ‘release’, ‘drawing’).

Type:

str

strategy

Used when a specific processing logic overrides standard behaviour (e.g., ‘consolidated’).

Type:

Optional[str]

Time Calculation Rules

src.core.time_calc.get_next_working_time(current_time: datetime, duration_minutes: int, holidays: Set[date], working_start_hour: int = 8, working_end_hour: int = 16) → datetime[source]

Calculates the end time by advancing current_time by duration_minutes, respecting working hours, skipping weekends and holidays.

Parameters:

  • current_time (datetime) – The starting datetime.

  • duration_minutes (int) – How many working minutes to advance.

  • holidays (Set[date]) – Set of dates to skip.

  • working_start_hour (int, optional) – Start of the working day. Defaults to WORKING_START_HOUR.

  • working_end_hour (int, optional) – End of the working day. Defaults to WORKING_END_HOUR.

Raises:

  • ValueError – If working_start_hour >= working_end_hour.

  • ValueError – If duration_minutes is negative.

Returns:

The resulting datetime after duration has accumulated.

Return type:

datetime

src.core.time_calc.is_working_day(day: date, holidays: Set[date]) → bool[source]

Checks if a given day is a working day, skipping weekends and holidays.

Parameters:

  • day (date) – The date to check.

  • holidays (Set[date]) – A set containing all known holiday dates.

Returns:

True if it is a working weekday, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

Schedule Engine

src.schedule.engine.calculate_task_dates(tasks: List[Task], project_start_date: datetime, holidays: Set[date], num_resources: int, working_start_hour: int = 8, working_end_hour: int = 16) → None[source]

Calculates the initiation and end dates for all tasks in the given list, respecting dependencies, working days/hours, and resource availability limits.

        graph TD
    A[Start] --> B["Reset Tasks Slack/Dates"]
    B --> C["Identify Ready Tasks<br/>(In-Degree = 0)"]
    C --> D{Ready Queue?}
    D -- Yes --> E["Pop task with min earliest_start"]
    E --> F{Resource Available?}
    F -- Yes --> G[Schedule Task]
    G --> H["Update Successors<br/>Decrement In-Degree"]
    H --> D
    F -- No --> I[Wait for next task finish]
    I --> D
    D -- No --> J["Backward Pass: Critical Path"]
    J --> End

The scheduling process uses Kahn’s algorithm for topological sorting. A task’s In-Degree is the number of its unfinished predecessors. When a task’s in-degree reaches 0, it means all its dependencies are satisfied.

Parameters:

  • tasks (List[Task]) – The overall project task sequence graph. These instances are mutated in place.

  • project_start_date (datetime) – Baseline timestamp for project progression.

  • holidays (Set[date]) – Days that will be entirely avoided when counting working time.

  • num_resources (int) – System-wide constraints setting parallel task limit execution.

  • working_start_hour (int, optional) – Factory shift start. Defaults to 8.

  • working_end_hour (int, optional) – Factory shift end. Defaults to 16.

Raises:

  • RuntimeError – If Kahn’s algorithm detects cycles in the task dependencies.

  • RuntimeError – If the scheduler inexplicably hangs without advancing all items.

Loader Handlers

src.schedule.loader.load_customization_types(file_path: Path) → List[CustomizationType][source]

Reads customization types from a CSV file, constructs their file paths, and caches them in memory.

Parameters:

file_path (Path) – Path to the customization_overview.csv file.

Returns:

A list of resolved CustomizationType configurations.

Return type:

List[CustomizationType]

src.schedule.loader.load_holidays(file_path: Path) → Set[date][source]

Loads holidays from a CSV file into a set of date objects.

Parameters:

file_path (Path) – Path to the holidays.csv file.

Returns:

A set containing all valid parsed holiday dates.

Return type:

Set[date]

src.schedule.loader.load_project_requirements(file_path: Path)[source]

Reads project requirements from a JSON file.

Supports two formats:

  • New: { “settings”: {…}, “milestones”: […] }

  • Legacy: […] (flat array of milestone dicts)

Parameters:

file_path (Path) – Path to the project requirements JSON file.

Returns:

A tuple containing:

  • settings (dict): Global project configuration settings.

  • milestones (List[Dict]): The list of configured milestones.

Return type:

tuple

src.schedule.loader.load_raw_tasks_from_csv(task_csv_path: Path) → List[Task][source]

Loads all tasks from the CSV file into raw Task objects.

This is used to create a template of tasks before any milestone-specific processing.

Parameters:

task_csv_path (Path) – Path to the deliverable_structure.csv file.

Returns:

A sequential list of un-linked baseline Task templates.

Return type:

List[Task]

src.schedule.loader.read_customization_duration(lookup_dict: Any, customization_name: str, customization_value: str, task_name: str, task_type_desc: str) → int | None[source]

Reads the duration from a specific customization cached Hash Map.

Parameters:

  • lookup_dict (Any) – The cached dictionary containing the CSV configurations.

  • customization_name (str) – The customization tag.

  • customization_value (str) – The value selected for the customization.

  • task_name (str) – The target task name.

  • task_type_desc (str) – The target task type.

Returns:

The parsed duration in minutes. None if not matched.

Return type:

Optional[int]

Project Schedule

class src.schedule.project.ProjectSchedule(project_requirements_path: Path, num_resources: int = 1, customization_overview_csv_path: Path | None = None, holidays_path: Path | None = None, project_start_date: datetime | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Orchestrates full schedule computing: loads, customises, schedules and consolidates tasks.

        graph TD
    A[Load project_config.json] --> B[Load deliverable_structure.csv]
    B --> C{For Each Milestone}
    C --> D[Process Variants & Duplication]
    D --> E[Apply Customizations]
    E --> F[Consolidate Drawing Tasks]
    F --> G[Bridge Zero-Duration Tasks]
    G --> H[Engine: Calculate Dates]
    H --> I[Finish]
num_resources

Maximum default resources acting in parallel.

Type:

int

project_requirements_path

Path to the requirements dataset.

Type:

Path

project_requirements_data

Loaded requirements payload.

Type:

List[Dict[str, Any]]

holidays

Loaded non-working days.

Type:

Set[datetime.date]

working_start_hour

Start of the working shift.

Type:

int

working_end_hour

End of the working shift.

Type:

int

milestones

Built sequence of project milestones.

Type:

List[ProjectMilestone]

tasks

The master flat list of instantiated tasks.

Type:

List[Task]

transformation_log

Audit trailing for debugging structure transformations.

Type:

List[Dict[str, Any]]

customization_types

Cached strategy overlays.

Type:

List[CustomizationType]

get_deliverable_end_date() → datetime | None[source]
get_deliverable_init_date() → datetime | None[source]
get_total_duration() → timedelta | None[source]

Exporters

src.export.csv_export.export_tasks_to_csv(schedule: ProjectSchedule, file_path: str)[source]

Exports all tasks with their related information to a CSV file.

Parameters:

  • schedule (ProjectSchedule) – The fully computed project schedule to export.

  • file_path (str) – The output file path.

src.export.csv_export.update_customization_overview_csv(file_path: Path)[source]

Reads customization_overview.csv, adds ‘path’ and ‘status’ columns, and writes the updated DataFrame back to the CSV.

Parameters:

file_path (Path) – Path to the customization_overview.csv file.

src.export.mermaid.export_tasks_to_mermaid_gantt(schedule: List[ProjectMilestone], output_file_path: Path | None = None, detail_level: str = 'full') → str[source]

Generates a Mermaid Gantt chart representation of tasks, grouped by milestone.

Parameters:

  • schedule (List[ProjectMilestone]) – The grouped sequence of milestones and tasks.

  • output_file_path (Optional[Path], optional) – Where to write the syntax file. Defaults to None.

  • detail_level (str, optional) – Export abstraction level (‘full’, ‘type’, ‘milestone_type_summary’). Defaults to ‘full’.

Raises:

ValueError – If detail_level is not recognized.

Returns:

The raw Mermaid Gantt syntax string.

Return type:

str

src.export.mermaid.export_tasks_to_mermaid_graph(schedule: List[ProjectMilestone], output_file_path: Path | None = None, detail_level: str = 'full') → str[source]

Generates a Mermaid flowchart (graph TD) representation of tasks, grouped by milestone.

Can generate a detailed graph of individual tasks or a high-level graph based on task types.

Parameters:

  • schedule (List[ProjectMilestone]) – The grouped sequence of milestones and tasks.

  • output_file_path (Optional[Path], optional) – Where to write the syntax file. Defaults to None.

  • detail_level (str, optional) – Graph abstraction level (‘full’, ‘type’, or ‘milestone’). Defaults to ‘full’.

Raises:

ValueError – If detail_level is not recognized.

Returns:

The raw Mermaid syntax string.

Return type:

str

src/export/gantt_interactive.py

Generates a self-contained interactive HTML Gantt chart from a list of Tasks. Uses Vis.js Timeline (loaded from CDN) — no extra Python dependencies.

Layout:

  • Rows = unique task names (e.g. “core_door”, “ply_adjustable_shelf”, …)

  • Bars = individual tasks, colour-coded by milestone

  • Sidebar panel 1: Milestone filter (toggle visibility per milestone colour)

  • Sidebar panel 2: Task-type filter (part_model / part_list / release / drawing …)

Features:

  • Pan / zoom / fit-all / today

  • Hover tooltip with full task detail

  • Export to JSON and CSV from the browser

src.export.gantt_interactive.export_interactive_gantt(tasks: List[Task], output_path: Path, title: str = 'Chrono Tailoring — Interactive Gantt Chart', milestone_name_map: Dict[Any, str] | None = None, total_resources: int = 1, project_requirements_path: Path | None = None, holidays: Set[date] | None = None, show_task_arrows: bool = False) → None[source]

Serialises tasks to JSON and writes a self-contained Vis.js HTML Gantt.

Rows = unique task names (swim-lanes). Items = individual task instances, coloured by milestone.

Parameters:

  • tasks (List[Task]) – Flat list of Task objects with init_date / end_date set.

  • output_path (Path) – Where to write the .html file.

  • title (str, optional) – Browser title / header text. Defaults to “Chrono Tailoring — Interactive Gantt Chart”.

  • milestone_name_map (Optional[Dict[Any, str]], optional) – Mapping of milestone IDs to names. Defaults to None.

  • total_resources (int, optional) – The total number of parallel resources applied. Defaults to 1.

  • project_requirements_path (Optional[Path], optional) – Path to requirements for the info sidebar. Defaults to None.

  • holidays (Optional[Set[date]], optional) – Dates to format differently. Defaults to None.

  • show_task_arrows (bool, optional) – Auto-enable standard dependency arrows on load. Defaults to False.

src.export.plot.plot_resource_vs_duration(base_schedule: ProjectSchedule, min_resources: int = 1, max_resources: int = 10, output_plot_path: Path | None = None)[source]

Runs scheduling for multiple resource counts, collects total durations, and plots the results.

Parameters:

  • base_schedule (ProjectSchedule) – The project schedule configuration.

  • min_resources (int, optional) – The minimum resources to simulate. Defaults to 1.

  • max_resources (int, optional) – The maximum resources to simulate. Defaults to 10.

  • output_plot_path (Optional[Path], optional) – Where to save the generated scatter plot. Defaults to None.
